title: Block Uniformity Audit
research_question: Why do we assume every block must contain both token mixing and an FFN, and can the model benefit from stage-specific or periodic heavy blocks instead of one uniform block recipe?
implementation_notes: Implemented via BLOCK_MODE=custom and per-layer BLOCK_TYPES.
Supports mixer_only, mixer_light, light, heavy, and standard block recipes.
Removed FFN sublayers now skip the MLP residual path explicitly rather than leaving a silent no-op.

candidates.name: z1-lower-two-mixer-only
candidates.goal: Ask whether the earliest lower-stack blocks need any FFN at all.
candidates.hypothesis: The first lower mixer-heavy blocks may only need sequence mixing, not full per-token FFN work.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the block recipe is already too heavy in the earliest lower stage.
candidates.why_worthy: This is one of the cleanest childlike questions in the whole model.
candidates.description: turn the first two lower mixer blocks into mixer-only blocks with no FFN sublayer

candidates.name: z2-lower-four-mixer-only
candidates.goal: Push the mixer-only idea through the full lower mixer stage.
candidates.hypothesis: Once the lower stack is already mixer-heavy, its FFNs may be largely redundant.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether lower-stage block simplification scales beyond a timid first step.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly tests whether the current lower-four hybrid still overpays for uniform per-block FFNs.
candidates.description: make all four lower mixer layers mixer-only blocks with no FFN sublayer

candidates.name: z3-alternating-full-and-mixer-only-lower
candidates.goal: Test whether heavy nonlinear refinement only needs to happen periodically.
candidates.hypothesis: The lower stack may only need a full FFN every other layer rather than after every mixer step.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether compute should be periodic rather than uniform.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the best first test of concentrated rather than evenly distributed block compute.
candidates.description: alternate standard and mixer-only blocks through the lower mixer stage

candidates.name: z4-lower-light-upper-full-blocks
candidates.goal: Test whether lower and upper stages deserve different block recipes.
candidates.hypothesis: The lower stack is mainly building structure, while upper layers do the expensive per-token refinement.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether stage specialization beats one uniform block template.
candidates.why_worthy: This follows the strongest repo-wide theme directly rather than treating all layers as equivalent.
candidates.description: use MLP-light blocks in the lower stack and full standard blocks in the upper stack

candidates.name: z5-periodic-heavy-blocks
candidates.goal: Concentrate heavy computation into fewer layers instead of spreading it evenly.
candidates.hypothesis: Capacity may be better concentrated into fewer heavy blocks than paid in every block.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the model wants periodic heavy refinement rather than uniform mid-strength blocks.
candidates.why_worthy: This questions one of the most standard transformer assumptions in a mechanically clean way.
candidates.description: use periodic heavy full blocks every two layers and lighter blocks elsewhere
